  "critique": "The note proves a genuine matched expected-diameter rate only for vanishing schedules satisfying the stated nuisance-product condition, with the converse additionally restricted to least-favorable-compatible primitives and c_G <= 3/16. Within that scope, the achievability and two-point converse quantify over the same directional shell and establish matching order (nr_n)^(-1/2); the lower theorem labeled conj:gaussian-lower is actually proved, not deferred. The bounded-count result proves only a nonshrinking lower obstruction on a separate capped comparison shell, not an upper procedure or a Poisson transition law. The framing accurately discloses the rate-only nature, the converse restrictions, and the absence of results for nonvanishing or oscillating schedules, so it does not materially overclaim what the statements establish.",
